snappySummary: An absolute circus. Almiron got sent off within forty minutes for swearing at the ref. Paraguay held on for a chaotic 1-0 win.
talkingPoints: Galarza scores the opener in 64 seconds;Almiron's red card completely derailed Türkiye's defensive blueprint;Paraguay's physical display ran the clock down effectively
randomQuirk: Almiron covering his mouth to swear at the referee, only to get caught by high-definition cameras.

snappySummary: Morocco danced through the Scottish midfield and claimed a deserved 2-1 victory. Scotland's late surge was too little, too late.
talkingPoints: Morocco's liquid football carved open the Scottish double-decker bus;Conceded to an overhead kick that defied all laws of Scottish gravity;Morocco's defense stood tall under late aerial pressure
randomQuirk: A Scottish fan in a kilt spotted reading a tactical guidebook upside-down in the 88th minute.

snappySummary: The USA cruised to a 3-0 victory over Australia. The Socceroos looked like they were having a casual kickabout in the park.
talkingPoints: Pulisic was a walking cheat code, scoring two and assisting one;Australia completely bottled it in possession, committing 18 turnovers;The high press from the Americans left Australia gasping for air
randomQuirk: An Australian defender trying to clear the ball, only to kick it directly into his own teammate's face.

snappySummary: Brazil dominated Haiti 5-0 in a match that felt like a training session. Vinicius Jr scored a hat-trick of pure class.
talkingPoints: Vinicius Jr put on a show with three goals and endless flair;Haiti's Sunday League defending conceded three goals from simple corners;Brazil hit the post three times, preventing an even bigger scoreline
randomQuirk: A Haitian defender asking Vinicius Jr to swap shirts during a water break in the 30th minute.

snappySummary: The Netherlands absolutely ran riot against Sweden, putting five past them in a performance that was more of a casual kickabout in the park for the Dutch.
talkingPoints: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo both bagging braces before the hour mark? Talk about liquid football, eh? Sweden's defence was more porous than a tea bag.;Sweden managed to pull one back, bless 'em, but conceding five goals and picking up three yellow cards suggests they spent more time fouling than playing.;The Dutch had fewer shots but more on target, showing clinical finishing, while Sweden's 16 shots for one goal is just a bit of a waste, isn't it?
randomQuirk: Sweden's keeper, Kristoffer Nordfeldt, made two saves, which is quite impressive considering he let in five. Must've been some absolute screamers flying past him!

snappySummary: Germany left it late but managed to claw back a win against Côte d'Ivoire, proving that sometimes you just need to keep plugging away, even if it's not pretty.
talkingPoints: Franck Kessie gave Côte d'Ivoire a shock lead, making Germany look a bit flustered for a while. You love to see it, don't you?;Deniz Undav, coming off the bench, clearly had his Weetabix this morning, bagging a brace including the winner in injury time. Super sub, that.;Germany dominated possession and shots, but needed a late, late show to avoid dropping points. Bit squeaky bum time for the home crowd, I imagine.
randomQuirk: Germany had three substitutions at the 60-minute mark, clearly a tactical masterclass or a desperate plea for someone, anyone, to score!

editionTitle: The Goalless Gala of Missed Opportunities
snappySummary: Ecuador and Curaçao played out a thrilling 0-0 draw, if by 'thrilling' you mean 'a match where both teams forgot where the goal was'.
talkingPoints: Ecuador had 74.6% possession and a staggering 27 shots with 15 on target, yet couldn't find the net. That's not just bottling it, that's completely emptying the bottle and then smashing it.;Curaçao's keeper, Eloy Room, was an absolute brick wall, making 15 saves. He probably deserves a medal, or at least a very large cup of tea.;Five yellow cards for Curaçao tells you they were more interested in stopping play than actually playing. A bit of a cynical masterclass, if you ask me.
randomQuirk: Curaçao managed zero corners despite their best efforts, proving that even when they got forward, they couldn't quite manage to threaten properly.
